title: gastrolobium formosum
intro: Gastrolobium formosum, also known as the Christmas tree or holly-leaved pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its leaves and stems are known for their medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of Gastrolobium formosum have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused in hot water to extract its medicinal benefits.
